eras.label: Dawn of Computing
eras.description: Mainframes, ARPANET, and the birth of the personal computer

eras.label: Personal Computer Revolution
eras.description: Home computers, game consoles, and the graphical OS

eras.label: The Web Takes Over
eras.description: Browsers, dot-com, Flash, ActionScript, and the open web

eras.label: Mobile Revolution & Flash Twilight
eras.description: iPhone changes everything; HTML5 rises as Flash fades

eras.label: The JavaScript Renaissance
eras.description: React, Node, Polymer, TypeScript — JS rules the stack

eras.label: The AI Age
eras.description: LLMs, AI coding assistants, and the next frontier

quotes.text: Time. Time doesn't pass. The passage of time is an illusion. And life is the magician.
quotes.author: Steve Moffat

quotes.text: People assume that time is a strict progression of cause to effect, but actually, from a non-linear, non-subjective viewpoint, it's more like a big ball of wibbly-wobbly, timey-wimey... stuff.
quotes.author: The Doctor — Doctor Who

quotes.text: Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic.
quotes.author: Arthur C. Clarke

quotes.text: The best way to predict the future is to invent it.
quotes.author: Alan Kay

quotes.text: Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it's bad.
quotes.author: Cory House

quotes.text: The Internet is the world's largest library. It's just that all the books are on the floor.
quotes.author: John Allen Paulos

quotes.text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
quotes.author: John Johnson

quotes.text: I am not a great programmer; I am just a good programmer with great habits.
quotes.author: Kent Beck

nodes.title: ARPANet Goes Live
nodes.description: The precursor to the internet — the first message sent between UCLA and Stanford. The seed of everything that follows.

nodes.title: C Programming Language
nodes.description: Dennis Ritchie releases C at Bell Labs. Nearly every language and OS that follows is built on top of it.

nodes.title: Pong — The First Arcade Hit
nodes.description: Atari's Pong debuts in arcades. The video game industry is born.

nodes.title: Microsoft Founded
nodes.description: Bill Gates and Paul Allen found Microsoft in Albuquerque, NM. The personal computing era begins.

nodes.title: Apple Computer Founded
nodes.description: Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne found Apple. The Apple I is their first product.

nodes.title: Atari 2600 Released
nodes.description: The Atari 2600 brings programmable cartridge gaming to living rooms everywhere. A future WSP obsession.

nodes.title: WSP Is Born
nodes.description: Walter S. Pollard Jr. is born in October 1978. The journey begins.

nodes.title: IBM PC Released
nodes.description: The IBM Personal Computer launches, legitimizing the home computer market and setting the PC standard for decades.

nodes.title: WSP: Summer of Atari
nodes.description: Young WSP is deep into Atari gaming — the spark that ignites a lifelong love of technology and interactive media.

nodes.title: Commodore 64 Launched
nodes.description: The best-selling personal computer of all time ships. 8-bit computing enters millions of homes.

nodes.title: Nintendo NES Launches in USA
nodes.description: Nintendo revives the video game market after the 1983 crash. Super Mario Bros. becomes a cultural phenomenon.

nodes.title: Windows 1.0 Released
nodes.description: Microsoft ships the first version of Windows. The graphical OS era begins.

nodes.title: World Wide Web Invented
nodes.description: Tim Berners-Lee proposes the World Wide Web at CERN. HTTP, HTML, and the browser are about to change everything.

nodes.title: Game Boy Released
nodes.description: Nintendo's portable console ships in Japan — gaming goes wherever you go.

nodes.title: Linux Kernel Released
nodes.description: Linus Torvalds posts the Linux kernel. Open source OS begins its march to power the internet.

nodes.title: Super Nintendo (SNES) Launched
nodes.description: The 16-bit era peaks with the Super Nintendo — Mode 7, Street Fighter II, and an era-defining library.

nodes.title: Mosaic Browser Released
nodes.description: The first graphical web browser — images alongside text. The web becomes visually accessible to the world.

nodes.title: Doom Released
nodes.description: id Software's Doom defines first-person shooters and PC gaming culture. Shareware distribution model disrupts everything.

nodes.title: PlayStation 1 Released (Japan)
nodes.description: Sony enters the console market with the PS1, introducing CD-ROM gaming and 3D graphics at scale.

nodes.title: JavaScript Created
nodes.description: Brendan Eich creates JavaScript at Netscape in 10 days. Initially called Mocha, then LiveScript. The language that defines WSP's career is born.

nodes.title: Java 1.0 Released
nodes.description: Sun Microsystems releases Java. "Write once, run anywhere" — the promise that heralds platform-independent computing.

nodes.title: Windows 95 Released
nodes.description: Start menu, taskbar, 32-bit support. Windows 95 puts 90 million new users on the PC and eventually the internet.

nodes.title: FutureWave Flash 1.0
nodes.description: FutureWave Software releases SmartSketch as FutureSplash Animator — soon acquired by Macromedia and rebranded Flash. A technology WSP will master and ride for 15+ years.

nodes.title: CSS Level 1 Standardized
nodes.description: The W3C standardizes CSS1, separating presentation from content. Web design enters a new era.

nodes.title: Nintendo 64 Released
nodes.description: The Nintendo 64 ushers in true 3D gaming. Super Mario 64 and Ocarina of Time redefine what games can be.

nodes.title: Drexel University — EECS
nodes.description: WSP enrolls at Drexel University studying Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. Begins writing code seriously.

nodes.title: ECMAScript 1 Standardized
nodes.description: JavaScript gets an official spec. Ecma International publishes ECMA-262, the foundation of modern JS.

nodes.title: IBM Deep Blue Beats Kasparov
nodes.description: IBM's Deep Blue defeats world chess champion Garry Kasparov in a rematch. Machines demonstrate superhuman performance on a cognitive task for the first time.

nodes.title: Google Founded
nodes.description: Larry Page and Sergey Brin incorporate Google Inc. PageRank changes how people discover content on the web.

nodes.title: Zelda: Ocarina of Time
nodes.description: Widely considered the greatest game ever made at release. 3D adventure gameplay sets a new bar.

nodes.title: First Dev Job — Unisys
nodes.description: WSP joins Unisys in Malvern, PA as a Web Developer and Systems Test Engineer. The career begins — while still finishing his Drexel degree.

nodes.title: Flash 4 — ActionScript Basics
nodes.description: Macromedia Flash 4 introduces primitive ActionScript. Scripted web interactivity begins. WSP enters the Flash ecosystem at exactly the right moment.

nodes.title: Flash 5 — ActionScript 1.0
nodes.description: Macromedia Flash 5 ships with ActionScript 1.0 — a proper scripting language. WSP begins writing AS. The Flash golden age begins.

nodes.title: Dot-Com Bubble Bursts
nodes.description: NASDAQ peaks and crashes. $5 trillion in market value evaporates. The industry consolidates — only the serious survive.

nodes.title: Wikipedia Launched
nodes.description: Jimmy Wales and Larry Sanger launch Wikipedia. Collaborative, open-source knowledge at scale.

nodes.title: Drexel University — Graduation
nodes.description: WSP graduates from Drexel University with a degree in Electrical Engineering / Computer Science.

nodes.title: Xbox (Original) Launches
nodes.description: Microsoft enters the console wars with the original Xbox. Halo: Combat Evolved ships at launch and becomes a phenomenon.

nodes.title: Flash MX — ActionScript 2.0
nodes.description: Macromedia Flash MX introduces ActionScript 2.0 with classes and OOP. Professional Flash development becomes serious engineering.

nodes.title: Cittone Institute — Network Systems
nodes.description: WSP completes a Network Systems Professional certification at Cittone Institute alongside his work at Unisys.

nodes.title: Facebook Launched
nodes.description: Mark Zuckerberg launches "The Facebook" from his Harvard dorm. The social media era begins in earnest.

nodes.title: Firefox 1.0 Released
nodes.description: Mozilla's Firefox launches, challenging IE6's dominance. The browser wars heat up — and web standards start to matter.

nodes.title: Flash Developer — Vantage Inc
nodes.description: WSP joins Vantage Inc in New Hope, PA as a Flash Developer. ActionScript is now the core of his craft.

nodes.title: World of Warcraft Released
nodes.description: Blizzard's World of Warcraft launches and defines the MMORPG genre. At peak it had 12 million subscribers.

nodes.title: YouTube Launched
nodes.description: Three former PayPal employees launch YouTube. Online video explodes — Flash video players are now everywhere.

nodes.title: Ajax Coined by Jesse James Garrett
nodes.description: Jesse James Garrett coins "Ajax" (Asynchronous JavaScript and XML). Dynamic web apps without full page reloads — this changes everything.

nodes.title: Flash 9 — ActionScript 3.0
nodes.description: Adobe (now owning Macromedia) ships Flash 9 with ActionScript 3.0 — a strongly typed, class-based language. WSP masters AS3 and rides it to the top of his career.

nodes.title: jQuery 1.0 Released
nodes.description: John Resig releases jQuery. Cross-browser DOM manipulation in one library — it becomes the most widely used JS library in history.

nodes.title: iPhone Announced
nodes.description: Steve Jobs unveils the original iPhone. "An iPod, a phone, and an internet communicator." Mobile computing changes forever — and Flash has no place on it.

nodes.title: Sr. Flash Developer — FEARnet / Comcast Entertainment
nodes.description: WSP joins FEARnet at Comcast Entertainment Group as Senior Flash Developer and Technology Leader. Leads front-end development across CSS, HTML, and Flash — Brightcove video, Omniture analytics, and DoubleClick DART ads.

nodes.title: Android 1.0 Released
nodes.description: Google ships Android 1.0 on the HTC Dream. The mobile OS wars begin — iOS vs Android will define the next decade.

nodes.title: Google Chrome + V8 Engine
nodes.description: Google launches Chrome with the V8 JavaScript engine. JS performance leaps forward — the stage is set for server-side JS.

nodes.title: Flash Developer — E! Online / Comcast Entertainment
nodes.description: WSP transitions from FEARnet to E! Online within Comcast Entertainment Group in Los Angeles, bringing Flash video and advertising expertise to the E! Entertainment platform.

nodes.title: Node.js Created
nodes.description: Ryan Dahl creates Node.js, bringing JavaScript to the server. The full-stack JS era begins.

nodes.title: Sr. Flash Developer — Razorfish Health
nodes.description: WSP joins Razorfish Health in the Philadelphia area as a Senior Flash Developer. AS3 server-side data feed integration and healthcare client applications.

nodes.title: Freelance — Sierra Star Studio
nodes.description: WSP runs Sierra Star Studio — a freelance full-stack web development practice alongside his contracted roles. Running parallel through multiple positions.

nodes.title: iPad Launched — No Flash
nodes.description: Steve Jobs publishes 'Thoughts on Flash' — Apple will never support Flash. The device that completes Flash's death sentence. HTML5 video rises to fill the void.

nodes.title: AngularJS 1.0 Released
nodes.description: Google's AngularJS introduces two-way data binding and MVC patterns for web apps. The JavaScript framework wars begin.

nodes.title: Sr. Flash Developer — Empathy Lab
nodes.description: WSP joins Empathy Lab in Conshohocken, PA. Leads AS2/AS3/FLEX development for major clients including Rogers OnDemand (Akamai CDN + HDCore), Sprout Online, and UltraViolet (UVVU). Builds Android 2.2+ video player.

nodes.title: IBM Watson Wins Jeopardy!
nodes.description: IBM's Watson AI defeats Jeopardy! champions Ken Jennings and Brad Rutter. Natural language understanding at superhuman scale makes headlines worldwide.

nodes.title: Twitter Bootstrap Released
nodes.description: Twitter open-sources Bootstrap. CSS grids and responsive design go mainstream overnight.

nodes.title: Freelance Flash Dev — Constellation.tv
nodes.description: WSP works as a freelance Senior Flash Developer for Constellation.tv in New York City. Builds live HD video + web camera player using OSMF and Akamai (RTMP, HDN1, HDN2, SecureHD). Live group movie watching with camera support.

nodes.title: Sr. Front End Developer — FNS/Sungard → Comcast CIM
nodes.description: WSP joins PA Fidelity National Information Services (formerly Sungard) consulting at Comcast Interactive Media (CIM). JavaScript front-end work, Google Polymer framework, and Adobe Primetime player PSDK — 5+ million views/month. Leads directly to full-time hire.

nodes.title: Senior Software Engineer 4 — Comcast Technology
nodes.description: WSP joins Comcast Technology and Product Xperience full-time as Senior Software Engineer 4. 13-year tenure begins. Specializes in HD video player playback on desktop and Chromecast. Becomes SME for Xfinity Stream and builds debugging tools, ad integrations (VOD, SVOD, DAI), and Chromecast solutions serving millions.

nodes.title: Facebook IPO
nodes.description: Facebook goes public at a $104 billion valuation — the largest tech IPO at the time. Social media is now a global utility.

nodes.title: AlexNet — Deep Learning Breakthrough
nodes.description: AlexNet wins ImageNet with a 10.8% error rate drop over the previous best. The deep learning era begins in earnest. GPUs, neural nets, and big data converge.

nodes.title: React.js Released by Facebook
nodes.description: Jordan Walke at Facebook releases React.js. Virtual DOM, component model, JSX — the JavaScript UI paradigm shifts fundamentally.

nodes.title: Google Polymer / Web Components
nodes.description: Google introduces Polymer and the Web Components spec. WSP adopts it at Comcast — building component-based TV UIs years before the mainstream catches on.

nodes.title: Google Acquires DeepMind
nodes.description: Google acquires DeepMind for ~$500M. The AI arms race between tech giants officially begins.

nodes.title: Flash End-of-Life Announced
nodes.description: Adobe officially announces Flash Player will be discontinued by December 2020. WSP had already pivoted to JS/TypeScript years earlier. An era ends.

nodes.title: AlphaGo Beats Lee Sedol at Go
nodes.description: DeepMind's AlphaGo defeats world Go champion Lee Sedol 4-1. Go was thought to be beyond AI for another decade. The world takes notice.

nodes.title: Pokémon GO Released
nodes.description: Niantic releases Pokémon GO — the first truly mass-scale AR game. 500M downloads in the first month. The real world becomes the game board.

nodes.title: "Attention Is All You Need" Paper
nodes.description: Google Brain's landmark paper introduces the Transformer architecture. Every major LLM — GPT, BERT, Claude — is built on this foundation.

nodes.title: Nintendo Switch Released
nodes.description: The Nintendo Switch ships — a hybrid home/handheld console. Zelda: Breath of the Wild at launch is a masterpiece. Nintendo proves innovation over power.

nodes.title: Flash Player Officially Discontinued
nodes.description: Adobe Flash Player reaches end-of-life on December 31, 2020. An era that powered the early web — and WSP's early career — is officially over. Long live JavaScript.

nodes.title: BERT Released by Google
nodes.description: Google's BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) sets a new bar for natural language understanding. Pre-trained models become the new paradigm.

nodes.title: GPT-2 by OpenAI
nodes.description: OpenAI releases GPT-2 — initially withholding the full model due to concerns about misuse. The quality of generated text shocks the research community.

nodes.title: COVID-19 — Remote Work Revolution
nodes.description: The global pandemic forces the tech industry fully remote overnight. Zoom, Slack, and cloud tools reach critical mass. The way software is built changes permanently.

nodes.title: GPT-3 by OpenAI
nodes.description: OpenAI's GPT-3 with 175 billion parameters produces eerily coherent text, code, and reasoning. The AI conversation shifts from research to product.

nodes.title: PS5 and Xbox Series X Launch
nodes.description: Sony and Microsoft launch their next-gen consoles amid a global chip shortage. SSD speeds and ray tracing redefine the generation.

nodes.title: GitHub Copilot Technical Preview
nodes.description: GitHub and OpenAI launch Copilot, an AI pair programmer trained on public code. Writing code will never feel the same. WSP adopts AI tooling as standard practice.

nodes.title: DALL-E 2 Released
nodes.description: OpenAI's DALL-E 2 generates photorealistic images from text prompts. Generative AI goes visual. Creative industries are disrupted overnight.

nodes.title: Elden Ring Released
nodes.description: FromSoftware and George R.R. Martin create Elden Ring. Universal critical acclaim. Open-world design and punishing gameplay redefine difficulty.

nodes.title: ChatGPT Released
nodes.description: OpenAI releases ChatGPT to the public. One million users in 5 days. One hundred million in two months. The AI revolution becomes impossible to ignore. The world changes.

nodes.title: GPT-4 Released
nodes.description: OpenAI's GPT-4 raises the bar again — multimodal, reasoning-capable, and able to pass bar exams. Every software team begins integrating LLMs.

nodes.title: Claude 1.0 by Anthropic
nodes.description: Anthropic releases Claude — a Constitutional AI assistant built with safety as a primary goal. The AI model powering this very portfolio's development.

nodes.title: Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
nodes.description: Sequel to Breath of the Wild adds a physics sandbox and sky islands. A landmark technical achievement on aging hardware.

nodes.title: The LLM Explosion
nodes.description: Llama 2 (Meta), Gemini (Google), Mistral, Falcon — open and closed LLMs proliferate. Every company ships an AI product. AI-assisted coding becomes the norm.

nodes.title: WSP Departs Comcast After 13 Years
nodes.description: After 13 years as Senior Software Engineer 4 at Comcast Technology — delivering HD video, Chromecast, ad systems, and debugging tools at global scale — WSP closes this chapter. Millions of views served. The next chapter begins.

nodes.title: Claude 3 — Opus, Sonnet, Haiku
nodes.description: Anthropic releases Claude 3 with three tiers. Opus surpasses GPT-4 on key benchmarks. The model family powering today's AI-assisted software engineering.

nodes.title: AI Coding Tools Go Mainstream
nodes.description: Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot Enterprise, Devin — AI coding assistants become standard in professional workflows. The definition of 'developer' expands.

nodes.title: WSP Builds Chromecast Portfolio
nodes.description: WSP builds this very website — a Next.js Chromecast Sender application showcasing 25+ years of software engineering experience. CI/CD, static export, Google Cast SDK, HLS/DASH streaming, and a nebula timeline. Built with Claude Code.

nodes.title: Claude 4.5 / 4.6 Released
nodes.description: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 — the model family driving Claude Code, the AI pair programmer used to build this portfolio.

nodes.title: You Are Here
nodes.description: March 2026. WSP is building the next chapter — open to new opportunities in video engineering, Chromecast, and AI-powered media experiences.

nodes.title: The Agentic AI Era
nodes.description: AI agents that plan, code, test, and deploy autonomously go from experimental to production-grade. Software engineering evolves again.

nodes.title: Spatial Computing Arrives
nodes.description: Mixed reality, Vision Pro successors, and spatial web experiences become mainstream development targets. WSP continues building at the cutting edge.

nodes.title: Berlin Wall Falls
nodes.description: The Berlin Wall — symbol of the Cold War division of Europe — is torn down. East and West Germany begin reunification. The Iron Curtain collapses.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Nintendo NES
nodes.description: Christmas morning delivers a Nintendo Entertainment System. Super Mario Bros., Duck Hunt, and a lifelong love of Nintendo begins.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Game Boy
nodes.description: Nintendo's portable gaming machine enters the picture. Tetris on the go — gaming goes everywhere.

nodes.title: Gulf War Begins
nodes.description: Operation Desert Storm launches as a US-led coalition drives Iraqi forces from Kuwait. The first major post-Cold War conflict, broadcast live on CNN.

nodes.title: Street Fighter II Hits Arcades
nodes.description: Capcom's Street Fighter II: The World Warrior drops in arcades and redefines competitive gaming. Ryu, Ken, Blanka, Chun-Li — a cultural phenomenon that owned every quarter in sight.

nodes.title: WSP: Summers at the Arcade
nodes.description: Every summer from 1991–1996, WSP rides his bike to the local mall arcade — and stretches $5 across 6 to 8+ hours of play. Street Fighter II, Mortal Kombat, NBA Jam. The arcade is a second home.

nodes.title: Soviet Union Dissolved
nodes.description: The USSR officially ceases to exist on December 25, 1991. The Cold War ends. Fifteen independent republics emerge, reshaping global geopolitics for decades.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Super Nintendo (SNES)
nodes.description: The 16-bit upgrade arrives. Street Fighter II on the SNES means bringing the arcade home. Mode 7, stellar soundtracks, and some of the greatest games ever made.

nodes.title: Nelson Mandela Elected — End of Apartheid
nodes.description: South Africa holds its first fully democratic elections. Nelson Mandela becomes the first Black president, ending decades of apartheid. One of the defining moments of the 20th century.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a PlayStation 1
nodes.description: Sony's PlayStation arrives. CD-ROM gaming, 3D polygons, and a library that includes Final Fantasy VII, Castlevania: SotN, and Metal Gear Solid. A next-level leap.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Game Boy Color
nodes.description: Nintendo's color handheld joins the collection. Pokémon Gold and Silver, Link's Awakening DX — portable gaming in full color.

nodes.title: September 11 Attacks
nodes.description: Al-Qaeda hijackers crash planes into the World Trade Center, the Pentagon, and a field in Pennsylvania. Nearly 3,000 killed. The world changes overnight — the War on Terror begins.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a PlayStation 2
nodes.description: The PS2 becomes the best-selling console of all time — and WSP has one. Grand Theft Auto III, God of War, Kingdom Hearts, Metal Gear Solid 2. An absurd library.

nodes.title: US Invades Iraq
nodes.description: The United States launches Operation Iraqi Freedom, invading Iraq under the claim of WMDs. Saddam Hussein's government falls. A conflict that reshapes the Middle East for decades.

nodes.title: Hurricane Katrina
nodes.description: Category 5 Hurricane Katrina devastates New Orleans and the Gulf Coast. Over 1,800 deaths, catastrophic flooding, and a massive failure of federal emergency response. One of the costliest natural disasters in US history.

nodes.title: WSP Gets an Xbox 360
nodes.description: Microsoft's 360 launches the HD gaming era. Halo 3, Gears of War, Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare — online multiplayer becomes mainstream. WSP is on Xbox Live.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a PlayStation 3
nodes.description: Sony's powerhouse console with built-in Blu-ray arrives in the collection. Uncharted, The Last of Us, Metal Gear Solid 4 — a golden era of cinematic gaming.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Nintendo Wii
nodes.description: Nintendo's motion-control revolution lands under the tree. Wii Sports, Super Mario Galaxy, Zelda: Twilight Princess — a totally different kind of fun.

nodes.title: Global Financial Crisis
nodes.description: Lehman Brothers collapses. The US housing bubble bursts, triggering a global financial meltdown. The worst economic crisis since the Great Depression — millions lose jobs and homes worldwide.

nodes.title: Barack Obama Elected President
nodes.description: Barack Obama is elected the 44th President of the United States — the first African American to hold the office. A historic moment for the country and the world.

nodes.title: Arab Spring Begins
nodes.description: A wave of pro-democracy protests erupts across the Arab world, starting in Tunisia. Egypt, Libya, Syria, and others follow. Social media plays a defining role in organizing resistance.

nodes.title: Osama bin Laden Killed
nodes.description: US Navy SEALs raid a compound in Abbottabad, Pakistan and kill Osama bin Laden, mastermind of the 9/11 attacks. Nearly a decade after 9/11, a chapter closes.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Nintendo Wii U
nodes.description: Nintendo's tablet-controller hybrid console joins the collection. Super Mario 3D World, Splatoon, and Breath of the Wild in its final days.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a PlayStation 4
nodes.description: The PS4 launches the current-gen era. Bloodborne, Horizon Zero Dawn, God of War (2018), Spider-Man — Sony's first-party studio output is unmatched.

nodes.title: Brexit — UK Votes to Leave the EU
nodes.description: The United Kingdom votes 52%–48% to leave the European Union, shocking the world. Years of political turmoil follow as the UK navigates its departure.

nodes.title: Donald Trump Elected President
nodes.description: Donald Trump wins the US presidential election in a stunning upset over Hillary Clinton, reshaping American politics and foreign policy.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Nintendo Switch
nodes.description: Nintendo's hybrid home/portable console is an instant classic. Breath of the Wild at launch, then Mario Odyssey, Metroid Dread, Tears of the Kingdom — the Switch library is legendary.

nodes.title: Philadelphia Eagles Win Super Bowl LII
nodes.description: THE moment. The Philadelphia Eagles defeat the New England Patriots 41–33 in Super Bowl LII — the franchise's FIRST Super Bowl championship. Nick Foles, the Philly Special, and pandemonium across the city. Fly Eagles Fly.

nodes.title: January 6 — US Capitol Attack
nodes.description: Supporters of President Trump storm the US Capitol building in an attempt to halt the certification of the 2020 election results. A defining and divisive moment in American democracy.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a PlayStation 5
nodes.description: After the notorious PS5 shortage, WSP secures Sony's next-gen beast. Demon's Souls, Ratchet & Clank: Rift Apart, and the DualSense controller that changes how games feel.

nodes.title: Russia Invades Ukraine
nodes.description: Russia launches a full-scale invasion of Ukraine, triggering the largest land war in Europe since World War II. The world rallies around Ukraine in a conflict that reshapes global security alliances.

nodes.title: Hamas Attacks Israel — Gaza War Begins
nodes.description: Hamas launches a large-scale attack on Israel on October 7, killing over 1,200 people and taking hundreds hostage. Israel's military response triggers a devastating war in Gaza and regional escalation.

nodes.title: Philadelphia Eagles Win Super Bowl LIX
nodes.description: The Eagles do it again — defeating the Kansas City Chiefs 40–22 in Super Bowl LIX. Back on top. Philly celebrates again. Fly Eagles Fly.

nodes.title: WSP Gets a Nintendo Switch 2
nodes.description: Nintendo's next-generation hybrid console arrives. The Switch lineage continues — bigger screen, more power, and a new generation of Nintendo magic.
